outcome_contract.why: Staff must manually scan each room timeline to find today's check-ins and check-outs; there is no summary. This creates friction during busy arrival/departure windows.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A "Today's Movements" section on the rooms-grid page lists arrivals (check-in date = today) and departures (check-out date = today) with room numbers and guest names, eliminating manual timeline scanning.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: DS primitives used throughout roomgrid; `OccupancyStrip` is closest analogue for layout
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No gap — follows same Inline/Stack pattern
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Confirm DS-only layout in component design

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `OccupancyStrip` only renders when `todayInWindow`; `UnallocatedPanel` hidden when empty
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Empty state (no arrivals/departures) must render gracefully — needs explicit text
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Define empty-state copy ("No arrivals today", "No departures today")

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Reception app is internal, access-controlled by operator login; no new data surface
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No new exposure — reads existing in-memory data

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No async operations, no mutations; pure read-only display derived from existing data
Jest + RTL pattern established; `OccupancyStrip.test.tsx` is the closest test pattern
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New component needs unit test file

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `TBookingPeriod.start` / `.end` are stable YYYY-MM-DD strings; `getReservationDataForRoom` API stable
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No schema changes — reads existing period fields
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Confirm `period.end === today` semantics (exclusive end means departure = today)

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Computation is O(rooms × beds × periods) — bounded by ~10 rooms × ~8 beds × few periods; no async
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No performance risk at this scale

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Reception app deploys as Cloudflare Worker; no feature flags present in codebase
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Rollback = revert commit; no migration or flag needed
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Plan for direct revert as rollback path

stage_payload.scope_summary: Add a compact "Today's Movements" panel to the `/rooms-grid` page that shows which guests are checking in today and which are checking out today. All required data is already available in `useGridData` — no new data fetching is needed.
goals: Surface today's arrivals (periods where `start === today`) and departures (periods where `end === today`) in one compact view.
Eliminate the need for staff to scan each room's timeline cell-by-cell.
Fit within the existing DS-primitive layout patterns used by `OccupancyStrip` and `UnallocatedPanel`.
non_goals: No new Firebase queries or data fetching.
No edit/action capability — read-only display only.
No historical movements (not yesterday, not tomorrow).
constraints: The `ds/enforce-layout-primitives` lint rule disallows raw flex/grid layout classes on leaf div elements — `Cluster`, `Inline`, and `Stack` from `@acme/design-system/primitives` must be used for layout. Plain `div`, `span`, `label` elements are permitted for semantics/accessibility, consistent with the rest of the roomgrid codebase.
Tests must be written but NOT run locally (CI-only per testing policy).
assumptions: `period.start` = check-in date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive); `period.end` = check-out date (YYYY-MM-DD, exclusive per `OccupancyStrip` convention).
"Departing today" = `period.end === today` (the guest's last occupied night was yesterday; they leave today).
Guest name can be absent (empty string); fall back to "Unknown".
key_entry_points: `apps/reception/src/components/roomgrid/RoomsGrid.tsx` — top-level rooms-grid page component; renders `StatusLegend`, `OccupancyStrip`, `UnallocatedPanel`, and one `RoomGrid` per room. This is the insertion point for `TodayMovements`.
